description: Golden contract for the weather-regime classifier (pipeline/regime.py — one implementation: it labels live readings and historical backfill in the pipeline, and the API imports it to label the live forecast). Both the pipeline and API test suites assert every case in this file, so any behaviour change must consciously update this spec. Inputs may be null (sensors go offline); a case's 'expected' is the regime code. Rules are evaluated worst-first; docs/weather-regimes.md explains each threshold's provenance.

inputFields.windGustMph: mph, from RWIS, NWS or Open-Meteo
inputFields.surfaceTempC: road surface °C, from RWIS (air temp proxy in forecasts)
inputFields.chainControl: Caltrans chain control status: null, R1, R2 or R3

cases.name: all inputs null → UNKNOWN
benign readings → CLEAR_DRY
single benign reading, rest offline → CLEAR_DRY (known data is benign)
heavy snowfall with low visibility → HEAVY_SNOW_LOW_VIS
boundary: snowfall exactly 0.5 and visibility just under 0.5 → HEAVY_SNOW_LOW_VIS
boundary: heavy snowfall but visibility exactly 0.5 → SNOW (not heavy)
light active snowfall → SNOW
boundary: trace snowfall under 0.1 with benign rest → CLEAR_DRY
chain control R1 means snow on the road even if sensors read dry → SNOW
chain control R3 → SNOW
cold road surface below black-ice threshold → ICE_FREEZING
boundary: surface exactly -4 °C is not yet the ice regime → CLEAR_DRY
snow outranks ice: snowing onto a frozen road → SNOW
gusts above 40 mph → HIGH_WIND
boundary: gusts exactly 40 mph → CLEAR_DRY (threshold is strict)
ice outranks wind: frozen road in a gale → ICE_FREEZING
low visibility without snow (fog or rain) → RAIN_FOG_LOW_VIS
boundary: visibility exactly 1 mile → CLEAR_DRY
wind outranks fog: gale with mist → HIGH_WIND
unrecognized chain control value is ignored → CLEAR_DRY
